updates.message: CPU functions on AWS went down.
We are investigating an issue in which Mounts are showing degraded performance.
Issue has been resolved. We are monitoring.

We are performing scheduled network maintenance related to web endpoint handlers. This is to improve overall stability of our web ingress system, and to prepare for a new system that will reduce the amount of "Bad Gateway" and "Internal Server Errors" that users experience.

We don't anticipate any downtime during this maintenance window, but we will stay alert and track any issues as they occur.

title: GPU functions on GCP and Web endpoints are down
Web API and Web endpoints are down

updates.message: We will be performing a scheduled upgrade.
We are experiencing higher load than usual leading to performance degradation across our APIs, function, webhook executions.
Issue resolved. Performance is back to normal.

title: GPU functions on GCP, GPU functions on OCI, API, Web endpoints, and 2 other services are down

updates.message: CPU functions on AWS and Web endpoints went down.
CPU functions on AWS, CPU functions on GCP, and CPU functions on OCI recovered.

From 23:30 UTC - 01:40 UTC, web endpoints deployed on Modal had degraded performance and transient "400 Bad Request" status codes. We responded to the issue and published a fix.

The root cause was increased load on our web endpoint system from user traffic, which tripped an internal rate limit.

title: Web API and Web endpoints are down

updates.message: Web endpoints went down and Web API recovered.
We'll conduct database upgrades for improved performance, stability, and security.

title: Frontend, Web API, and 1 other service are down

updates.message: According to our investigation this was a false positive. Independent internal monitoring reports no issues and we failed to reproduce any problems. We're continuing to investigate the root cause.

title: Frontend and Web API are down

updates.message: We are upgrading an internal database. The Modal service should remain available during this time, although response latencies and error rates might be somewhat elevated.

title: DNS issues causing website downtime and degradation of the service

updates.message:
We experienced a problem with our DNS records for modal.com and modal.run, meaning that the website and webpoints were unreachable for a period, unless DNS records were cached by your local resolver.

This also affected workloads running on modal to some degree, as some workloads were failing to communicate with our API server.

The issue has been resolved but there might be lingering effects due to negative DNS caches in your local DNS resolver (e.g. your ISP resolvers).
We will be upgrading one of our databases to improve performance and reliability.
We are migrating a database for better performance and reliability.
We will upgrade one of our databases to reduce latency and improve reliability.

We hit a limit on our internal workspace used to run synthetic monitors, which caused our downtime alerts to fire inaccurately. We have raised the limits now. 

No users were affected. We're sorry for all the spam for status page subscribers!
We’re experiencing database I/O load issues at the moment, which is affecting responsiveness on the modal.com dashboard and execution of function calls. We’re actively working on resolving the issue.
We are still experiencing degraded performance and reliability due to database load. However, we have found the root cause of the issue and are continuing to work toward resolution.
We are rebooting the database to apply an urgent configuration change. Minor disruption for up to 1 minute is expected.

We have resolved the issue. Thank you for your patience!

We are continuing to monitor.

title: CPU functions on AWS, CPU functions on GCP, and 4 other services are down

updates.message: CPU functions on AWS, CPU functions on GCP, CPU functions on OCI, and 2 other resources went down.
Web API went down and CPU functions on GCP and CPU functions on OCI recovered.
We're performing an infrastructure change on our website (modal.com). The dashboard may be inaccessible to some users for up to a minute.

title: CPU functions on GCP, Web endpoints, and 1 other service are down

updates.message: CPU functions on GCP and Volumes recovered.
We are seeing degraded performance on Modal function executions and are investigating.
We have identified the problem and are recovering at the moment.
The system has recovered, and function executions should be proceeding as normal. The team is on standby for any further issues.

title: Ephemeral apps and image builds are degraded.

updates.message: Trigger processing issues have caused issues in ephemeral Apps and modal.Image builds. We're investigating.

At 12:57 UTC a container filesystem change rolled out across workers which caused spurious 'file not found' (ENOENT) errors against the container's rootfs filesystem. 

We identified the bad commit and rolled back to prevent new workers from picking up the bug. 

We then worked to roll over all running production workers which were affected by the bad change and scale up new workers.

All affected workers have been quarantined and thus new containers will start on new, healthy workers. 

We apologize for the disruption.

title: CPU container performance degradation from over-scheduling on GCE n1-standard instance class

updates.message:
Between Monday 23rd 15:54 UTC and 15:36 UTC a change was made to make GCP workers poll more quickly for containers and this caused them to pick up more CPU-only containers.

Between 13:48 UTC and 16:18 UTC on September 24th CPU containers were allowed to be scheduled on GCP NVIDIA T4 workers which are the `n1` class of GCE VMs.

Both of these changes combined to cause over-scheduling of CPU work onto GCP T4 workers and significantly degrade the performance of the hosted containers (whether CPU-only or T4 GPU-enabled).

We are updating the DNS records of our website at modal.com to point to a new CDN deployment. We expect this to be a seamless transition with no observable downtime, but we are declaring a maintenance window as a precaution. (Your website will just get faster!)
We will be performing a scheduled upgrade.

title: Performance degradation across Functions, Sandboxes, and web endpoints

updates.message: We are experiencing performance degradation across all Functions and web endpoint calls.
The modal.com web dashboard is unavailable and returning 502s. We are investigating and believe we have diagnosed the problem.
We have resolved the issue. The modal.com dashboard has been back online since about 1:40 PM ET, 10 minutes ago. Deployments are stable again. We are continuing to monitor.
During this time, Modal is expected to work. There may be potential degraded performance (hopefully short-lived).
We are currently investigating this issue.

The web application and the model management API are operational. We continue to monitor the situation.

updates.message: We have a fix we're rolling out and monitoring
The API is back up, but we continue to monitor.
We are continuing to monitor for any further issues.
We've implemented a few layers of mitigation to handle this extended and ongoing DDOS. After hours of monitoring, the systems are stable right now.
New model builds are experiencing timeouts, preventing new models from being deployed. Existing model deploys and inference are not affected.
We've mitigated the impact on model builds and are still investigating the root issue. Existing model deploys and inference are not affected.

title: Volumes and Image builds are down

updates.message: We are investigating networking issues in some of our hardware, which is causing errors connecting TCP sockets on sandboxes.
We have identified networking issues with generalized user impact

We have identified the cause of the networking issue in Oracle Cloud. Oracle Cloud has confirmed an ongoing NOC outage in Oracle Cloud in us-ashburn-1. We are moving capacity to other cloud providers right now.

Oracle Cloud is mitigating the issue.

In the meantime, we're trying to help the autoscaler scale up instances as fast as possible in other clouds.

We have scaled up capacity successfully and redirected traffic to work around the Oracle Cloud regional networking outage in Ashburn, VA.

Newly created sandboxes are fully operational. Some sandboxes created during the incident may not have been able to spin up.

title: Cloudflare outage in us-east-1 causing Modal degradation in us-east-1

updates.message:
Update: The incident is now resolved. 

Cloudflare incident: https://www.cloudflarestatus.com/incidents/t8mq7d1yjqm4

We are remediating this by shifting Modal Workers away from us-east-1.

title: Elevated GPU device failures in us-east

updates.message: We have noticed limited elevated GPU device failures on L4 nodes in one of our US-east clusters. We have identified the issue and have a fix in place, we are monitoring for any impact to models.

title: GCP networking issue degrading GCP-pinned Modal containers

updates.message:
Due to network degradation in GCP, Modal is compromised in its ability to maintain Worker capacity and container health. 

If you have GCP-pinned (cloud="gcp") Functions or Sandboxes, unpinning will result in avoiding this issue.

title: A few A100 nodes are failing. Recovery is in progress.

updates.message: This is due to a single cloud provider's unplanned outage, which is impacting our A100 fleet. Recovery onto other clouds is in progress.

title: Noticing elevated inference latencies in one cluster

updates.message: We have identified the root cause: a networking issue within one cluster of one of our cloud providers.
Monitoring the fix that went in a few minutes ago.
Inference latencies are back to normal.
We will be conducting internal networking updates that affect our main API server.

title: TLS issue with web endpoints on new, custom domains

updates.message:
Due to an [active incident at Let's Encrypt](https://letsencrypt.status.io/), we are unable to issue SSL certificates for web endpoints on new, custom domains (eg., `yourwebsite.com`).

* New web endpoints using our default `modal.run` domain are unaffected.
* Existing web endpoints for which we have already issued certificates are unaffected.
* We renew certificates 30 days in advance, so we do not expect any certificates to expire.

We are actively testing a backup provider which we may bring online if the incident continues.
We have added a backup provider for SSL certificates, and issuance has resumed successfully.

Due to an internal service issue we observed degraded container scheduling during 8/12 5:38 UTC to 6:30 UTC. Containers took much longer than typical to come up, affecting new inputs. Existing containers continued serving traffic. This was resolved by 8/12 6:30 UTC.

title: Modal.com dashboards and image builds are degraded

updates.message:
Metrics dashboards on Modal.com are degraded: requests for metrics, logs, etc., are either slow or failing. Image build performance is also degraded.

We are working with a vendor to resolve downstream performance issues. The Modal runtime environment, including function execution, is unaffected.
We identified the issue and we're starting to see recovery for the dashboards and image build times.

title: A10G, L40S scheduling latency is high

updates.message:
Due to an inability to scale up workers in AWS A10G and L40S Function scheduling latency is degraded. Since 06:47 UTC today A10G scheduling latency has been heavily degraded. Since 06:20 UTC today L40S scheduling latency has been heavily degraded. 


As of 10:40 UTC scaling in AWS has been restored and queuing times are recovering as the worker pool grows its AWS A10 and L40S capacity.

As of 10:41 UTC scheduling latency has recovered on A10G and L40S.

Apologies for this incident and the customer impact caused. Please contact support to access the post-mortem document if interested.

title: L4 capacity in single us-east region having issues

updates.message: We have identified an issue with some of our US-based L4 capacity. We are working on bringing up additional capacity elsewhere to serve the demand.
Workloads have been migrated to new capacity and we are monitoring the situation.

title: Emails from modal.com are not sending

updates.message: Invite emails, notification emails and others aren't sending. We're currently investigating the issue.

title: Build Infrastructure degraded due to Dockerhub outage

updates.message: An ongoing incident with Dockerhub registries is affecting new model builds. All existing models are unaffected.
We are continuing to monitor for any further issues.

title: High latency on Baseten Web Application and Model Management APIs. Inference is unaffected.

updates.message: We are seeing elevated latency across our Web Application and Rest APIs. Inference is unaffected.

title: Modal Tunnels servers hitting certificate expiry

updates.message:
Clients attempting to connect to encrypted Modal Tunnels (https://modal.com/docs/guide/tunnels#tunnels) may experience connection failure due to certificate expiry that occurred at midnight October 13th UTC. 

We are working to restore health to encrypted Tunnels by removing hosts with expired certificates or refreshing certificates.
We have resolved the certificate expiration issue on all affected hosts.

AWS us-east-1 is experiencing an outage and many of our services are affected. We're looking at mitigating the impact.

We have mostly recovered alongside the recovery in AWS us-east-1, but continue to have sporadic connection issues against Redis (AWS Elasticache).
AWS has marked the underlying incident us-east-1 as recovered, and we've seen recovery across our affected services.
New logins are not working due to an upstream auth provider outage. Existing logins are unaffected by this. Inference, new deploys, training jobs, and the model management APIs are also unaffected.

New logins continue not work due to issues with our upstream auth provider. Existing logins are unaffected by this. Inference, new deploys, training jobs, and the model management APIs are also unaffected.
The issue with our auth provider has been fixed, and logins work again.

title: Config change causes container filesystem IO errors

updates.message:
An incorrect configuration change applies aggressive throttling on container filesystem bandwidth and is causing downtime. 

We have reverted the configuration and are managing recovery.
The rollback has completed and errors have subsided.

title: CPU functions, GPU functions, and 4 other services are down

updates.message: This is a false alarm. Sorry for the noise!
We're being throttled by an upstream service, which is affecting image builds / app deploys. We've identified the root cause and are fixing.

title: DNS servers flooded with MX records, causing DNS query timeouts

updates.message:
At 19:10 UTC today our DNS server system which is used by Sandboxes took on a flood of (invalid) MX traffic. 

By 19:50, we'd scaled up our DNS servers and the MX traffic spike plateaued, allowing DNS query latencies to recover.

DNS query latencies have recovered, per our internal synthetic monitoring. 

We apologies for this disruption. Please contact us for further details.

title: Deepseek V3-0324 on Model APIs is down. **Dedicated inference is not affected**.

updates.message: We are currently investigating the issue.
A fix has been implemented and the service has recovered. Continuing to monitor.

title: GPU Function queueing due to Tailscale outage

updates.message:
Due to an outage in a third-party provider, Tailscale, new Workers have been unable to scale since 8:54:37 UTC. 

We're are working to remove Tailscale as a dependency while it's down.
We have been successfully launching new Workers as of 3 minutes ago.
Queueing has been resolved. Apologies for the disruption.
We are having an issue with volumes, we are investigating.
Volume operations appear to be stable for now, we are actively monitoring and working on the underlying issue.

title: Slower than usual scale-ups and new deploys for all models in GCP

Indicators of scheduling latency have recovered.

title: Some services are down due to Database

updates.message: CPU functions, GPU functions, Snapshot restores (beta), and 2 other resources went down.
We are investigating and believe we have diagnosed the problem: we are having an active incident with our database and are actively working on mitigating.
We are currently in the process of figuring out the best mitigation plan that will minimize risk and downtime once it is complete.
We have escalated this issue to AWS, and also are working on restoring a copy of the database in parallel.
We have just finished spinning up a new database instance from backup and pointing traffic to it. We are seeing recovery for users, and are actively keeping a close eye on metrics.
All services operational as of 11:06 UTC.
We experienced a drop in capacity across our fleet.
We stopped the capacity loss and are beginning bringing back capacity.

The issue has been mitigated and normal access to Volumes via CLI, API and the dashboard is restored.

We refreshed the cert and the service has recovered.

title: Function input retrievable and output publishing degraded

updates.message:
Due to a Redis cluster node becoming saturated Function input fetching and output publishing has an elevated failure rate. 

We're actively working on remediation to bring down error rates to zero.
Error rate is back to zero and service is fully restored.

title: Elevated inference latencies in AWS us-west-2

updates.message: We are currently investigating this issue.
The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.
A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.
We are continuing to monitor for any further issues.
Qwen3 Embedding 8B embeddings API went down
All other input planes are now recovered.
Web endpoint requests were experiencing elevated latency and errors.

title: A fraction of new containers are experiencing errors

updates.message: We have identified the root cause and are taking action to mitigate.
Issues with some image builds getting stuck have been resolved.

New containers are working normally. We are continuing to investigate possible lingering effects for recently started containers.

At 13:30 UTC a bad change went out causing new containers to potentially get stuck or experience errors. At 14:44 UTC a the bad change was fully reverted and the disruption mitigated.

We experienced a secondary, lesser problem with similar symptoms between approximately 16:00 and 16:15 UTC as part of the mitigations put in place during the response.
Functions are fully restored. We are still investigating possible lingering effects for a small number of Sandboxes that were launched between 13:30-14:44 UTC.

title: Partial inference outage in AWS us-east-1

updates.message: We are currently investigating this issue.
We are continuing to investigate this issue.
A handful of models in this cluster are experiencing downtime while we migrate them out. Moving the status to partial outage due to this.

title: Elevated 500s on Model APIs. **Dedicated Inference is not affected**

updates.message: There are elevated 500s on Minimax, Kimi 2.5, Kimi K2 Thinking, Kimi K2 0905 on Model APIs.

Minimax and Kimi K2 0905 are now healthy. Kimi K2 Thinking and Kimi 2.5 are still seeing elevated 500 errors. The team has identified and is implemented a fix. **Dedicated inference is not affected**.
Kimi K2 Thinking is still experiencing elevated 500s. All other Model APIs are healthy.  **Dedicated Inference is unaffected**
Task logs have been delayed since 2:46 PM ET
We have identified and fixed a bottleneck; task logs are flowing through the system normally

updates.message: There was a backup in the deploy queue, resulting in some deploy failures.
There was a backup in the deploy queue, resulting in some deploy failures. Please retry any deploys from this period.

title: us-east input plane web endpoint degradation

updates.message: Some web endpoint requests that are proxied through our us-east input plane are failing.
Modal.com web dashboard degraded. Metrics are slower to load.
We have seen the service recover after the deploy.

title: Builds of new models are currently failing. **Inference is not affected**

updates.message: We are currently investigating this issue.
A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.
We are continuing to monitor for any further issues.
